agentScoring.title: Agent Scoring
agentScoring.subtitle: Proof-gated scoring — only signals with a proven historical edge (≥15 trades, ≥45% win rate) are scored. Unproven signals can be expanded via the Emerging toggle.
agentScoring.presetTooltipDesc: Each preset adjusts how the 4 AI agents are weighted when scoring trades.
agentScoring.analystTooltip: Bayesian win-probability & historical hit-rates for the detected pattern. Higher weight → prioritize setups with strong statistical edge.
agentScoring.riskTooltip: ATR-based stop placement, Kelly sizing & risk/reward quality. Higher weight → prioritize tight risk control with well-defined stops.
agentScoring.timingTooltip: Blends trend alignment (with_trend=0.85, neutral=0.55, counter=0.30) 50/50 with live economic calendar data. Penalizes entries near high-impact events (−0.15 per FOMC/NFP/CPI) and medium-impact (−0.06) within 48h, matched by currency to your instrument. Higher weight → avoid trading into scheduled volatility.
agentScoring.portfolioTooltip: Evaluates real portfolio exposure when basket symbols are selected: asset-class concentration, currency correlation (e.g. AUD/USD + NZD/USD overlap), and directional skew. Without basket context, falls back to pattern quality grade. Higher weight → enforce diversification.
agentScoring.addSymbolsPlaceholder: Add symbols or pick from table +
agentScoring.scoresUpdateLive: — scores update live as you adjust agents
agentScoring.noDetections: No active detections for this filter combination.
agentScoring.noDetectionsHint: Try changing the asset class or timeframe filter.
agentScoring.rrTooltip: Risk-to-Reward ratio. How much potential profit per unit of risk. Higher is better — 2.0+ is the baseline.
agentScoring.analystHeaderTooltip: 3-component score: Win Rate (0–10 pts), Expectancy R-multiple (0–10 pts), and Sample Confidence (0–5 pts, log₂ scale). Only scored for proven signals (≥15 trades, ≥45% win rate).
agentScoring.riskHeaderTooltip: 3-component score: R:R Adequacy (0–10 pts, 1.5 R:R = 5 pts), Volatility Stability (0–8 pts, tighter stops = higher), and Kelly Sizing (0–7 pts, capped at 25%). Evaluates position sizing feasibility.
agentScoring.timingHeaderTooltip: 50% trend alignment (with_trend=0.85, neutral=0.55, counter=0.30 via EMA, MACD, RSI, ADX) + 50% economic event calendar. High-impact events (−0.15 each) and medium (−0.06) within 48h for the instrument's currencies reduce this score. Clear calendar + with-trend = highest score.
agentScoring.portfolioHeaderTooltip: When basket has symbols: real concentration risk (asset-class overlap), currency correlation (shared USD/EUR/JPY exposure), and directional skew (too many longs/shorts). Without basket: quality grade proxy. Add symbols to basket for meaningful portfolio scoring.
agentScoring.scoreTooltip: Weighted composite score (0–100) combining all four agent scores. Only calculated for proven signals that pass the Proof Gate (≥15 historical trades with ≥45% win rate). Emerging signals are not scored.
agentScoring.verdictTooltip: TAKE (≥70) = actionable edge, WATCH (50–69) = monitor, SKIP (<50) = pass. Only proven signals receive a verdict. Signals without sufficient history are labeled UNPROVEN and separated into the Emerging section.
agentScoring.basketTooltip: Add symbols to the backtest basket. Selected symbols will auto-populate the Run Backtest input.
agentScoring.takeRateTooltip: Percentage of scanned setups that scored ≥70 (TAKE verdict). A high Take Rate means your current weights are accepting more trades — good for aggressive styles, but watch for quality dilution.
agentScoring.watchRateTooltip: Percentage of setups scoring 50–69 (WATCH verdict). These are borderline opportunities worth monitoring but not yet actionable. A high Watch Rate suggests many setups are close to your threshold.
agentScoring.skipRateTooltip: Percentage of setups scoring below 50 (SKIP verdict). A high Skip Rate means your agents are filtering aggressively — typical of conservative presets. This protects capital but may miss opportunities.
agentScoring.avgScoreTooltip: The mean composite score (0–100) across all scanned setups, weighted by your agent configuration. Compare this across presets to see how your weighting strategy shifts overall market sentiment.
agentScoring.addSymbolsFirst: Add symbols to the basket first. Click + next to rows in the table.
agentScoring.tooManyInstruments: Too many instruments ({{count}}). Maximum is {{max}} per run. Remove some from the basket.
agentScoring.noActivePatterns: No active patterns found for selected symbols. Pick symbols from the table.
agentScoring.signInToRun: Please sign in to run backtests
agentScoring.authGateFreeTitle: Create a free account to backtest
agentScoring.authGateDesc: You've used your free backtest. Sign in or create a free account to unlock more backtests and save your results.
agentScoring.authGateFreeDesc: Agent Scoring is free to use. Create a free account to run your first backtest and validate your trade ideas with historical data.
agentScoring.faqWhatAreDesc: The Multi-Agent Trade Filter uses four specialized AI agents that independently evaluate every trade opportunity. Before scoring, each signal must pass the Proof Gate — only setups backed by ≥15 historical trades with a ≥45% win rate are scored. Signals that don't meet this threshold are labeled UNPROVEN and shown in a separate Emerging Signals section. Agent Weights control how much influence each agent has on the final Composite Score (0–100) that determines whether a proven trade is labeled TAKE, WATCH, or SKIP.
agentScoring.faqProofGateDesc: The Proof Gate ensures you only act on signals with a verified statistical edge. A signal must have: (1) at least 15 resolved historical trades for that pattern + instrument combination, and (2) a win rate of 45% or higher. Signals failing either criterion are classified as Emerging — they are still visible but not scored, and their Analyst and Composite columns show '—' with an UNPROVEN badge. Send emerging signals to Pattern Lab to investigate their potential.
agentScoring.faqFormulaDesc: The four weights must sum to 100. Each agent's raw score (0–1) is multiplied by its weight, and the results are summed to produce the composite. A composite of 70+ defaults to TAKE, 50–69 to WATCH, and below 50 to SKIP (thresholds are adjustable). Only proven signals (those passing the Proof Gate) receive a composite score.

ascending-triangle.name: Ascending Triangle
ascending-triangle.description: A bullish continuation pattern formed by a flat resistance line and a rising support trendline, indicating increasing buying pressure.

descending-triangle.name: Descending Triangle
descending-triangle.description: A bearish continuation pattern with a flat support and declining resistance, suggesting sellers are gaining control.

symmetrical-triangle.name: Symmetrical Triangle
symmetrical-triangle.description: A neutral consolidation pattern with converging trendlines that can break in either direction, reflecting market indecision.

double-bottom.name: Double Bottom
double-bottom.description: A bullish reversal pattern that forms after a downtrend when price tests a support level twice and bounces, resembling the letter "W".

double-top.name: Double Top
double-top.description: A bearish reversal pattern where price tests a resistance level twice and fails to break through, forming an "M" shape.

triple-bottom.name: Triple Bottom
triple-bottom.description: A bullish reversal pattern with three roughly equal lows, indicating strong support and potential trend reversal.

triple-top.name: Triple Top
triple-top.description: A bearish reversal pattern where price tests resistance three times, signaling exhaustion and a likely downside break.

head-and-shoulders.name: Head and Shoulders
head-and-shoulders.description: A classic bearish reversal pattern with three peaks — the middle peak (head) is the highest, flanked by two lower peaks (shoulders).

inverse-head-and-shoulders.name: Inverse Head and Shoulders
inverse-head-and-shoulders.description: A bullish reversal pattern mirroring the head and shoulders, with three troughs where the middle is the deepest.

bull-flag.name: Bull Flag
bull-flag.description: A short-term bullish continuation pattern where a sharp upward move is followed by a brief rectangular consolidation before continuing higher.

bear-flag.name: Bear Flag
bear-flag.description: A bearish continuation pattern where a sharp decline is followed by a brief upward-sloping consolidation before resuming lower.

rising-wedge.name: Rising Wedge
rising-wedge.description: A bearish pattern where price converges within upward-sloping trendlines, typically breaking down as buying momentum fades.

falling-wedge.name: Falling Wedge
falling-wedge.description: A bullish pattern with downward-converging trendlines, usually resolving to the upside as selling pressure diminishes.

cup-and-handle.name: Cup & Handle
cup-and-handle.description: A bullish continuation pattern resembling a teacup, with a rounded bottom (cup) followed by a slight pullback (handle) before breakout.

inverse-cup-and-handle.name: Inverse Cup & Handle
inverse-cup-and-handle.description: A bearish variant of the cup and handle, with a rounded top followed by a small rally before breaking down.

donchian-breakout-long.name: Donchian Breakout (Long)
donchian-breakout-long.description: A momentum-based long signal triggered when price breaks above the upper Donchian channel, indicating a potential new uptrend.

donchian-breakout-short.name: Donchian Breakout (Short)
donchian-breakout-short.description: A momentum-based short signal triggered when price breaks below the lower Donchian channel, suggesting a potential new downtrend.
